parameters.content:
## Handle Data File and Form Data

Captures and extracts the submitted data and the binary data file uploaded for analysis so that it can be accessed correctly in later steps.

## Data Analysis

Step to analyze submitted Kindletrends Amazon top 100 bestseller data and provide insights, trends, etc. Middle step imports System message.

## Create Folder and Document

Step creates a Project folder based on the Project Nickname given in the form and creates a file o capture initial metadata information and AI analysis.

values.fieldLabel: Number of Spicy Scenes Per Book

=[BEGIN SYSTEM]
{{ $json.content }}
[END SYSTEM]

[BEGIN INSTRUCTIONS]
Analyze the [data] for patterns and trends, with a focus on series. Focus on the following: subgenres, settings, themes, tone and style, standalone vs. series, pricing, and accessibility.

Select the Most Successful Ingredients. Based on the data, pick the most popular and consistent elements.

Identify Gaps or Opportunities for Innovation. Look for opportunities to innovate by combining popular tropes or adding a fresh twist.

List a summary of 5-7 key trends.

[BEGIN DATA]
{{ $('Store KT Data').item.json.data }}
[END DATA]
[END INSTRUCTIONS]

=[BEGIN SYSTEM]
{{ $('System Instructions').last().json.content }}
[END SYSTEM]

[BEGIN INSTRUCTIONS]
Based on the [ANALYSIS] below, please create a list of 20 common or trending tropes that appear in the [genre]. 

Evaluate each trope based on: 
Popularity in the market.
Emotional intensity.
Drama factor.
Trend alignment.
Freshness potential.

Assign a score from 1 to 10 for each criterion.

Output: A table with tropes, scores for each criterion, and their total scores. Highlight the top 5 tropes.

[BEGIN SERIES DATA]
{{ $json.content }}
[END SERIES DATA]

[BEGIN DATA ANALYSIS]
{{ $('Get Analyses').item.json.content }}
[END DATA ANALYSIS]
[END INSTRUCTIONS]

=[BEGIN SYSTEM]
{{ $('System Instructions').last().json.content }}
[END SYSTEM]

{{ $('Get Analyses1').item.json.content }}

{{ $('Get Series Data').item.json.content }}

[BEGIN INSTRUCTIONS]

This series is a {{ $('On form submission1').last().json['Type of Project'] }}.

**Dynamic/Sequential Series (same characters)**
A multi-book series following the same core characters through an overarching plot that develops across all books. Each book advances the main story arc while containing its own complete plot. The premise must have sufficient complexity, escalating stakes, and character growth potential to sustain the planned number of books. Think Harry Potter or The Hunger Games.

**Anthology Series (interconnected different characters)**
A series set in the same world/universe where each book features different main characters, but all stories are interconnected through setting, themes, or background events. The premise must establish a rich world with multiple story possibilities and natural connection points between books. Each book stands alone but enriches the overall series experience. Think Black Dagger Brotherhood or Discworld.

**Static Series (character driven)**
A series featuring the same main character(s) in episodic adventures where each book is self-contained with minimal overarching plot development. The premise should focus on a compelling protagonist with a repeatable story framework (detective solving cases, spy on missions). Character relationships may evolve, but the core premise remains stable. Think Jack Reacher or Stephanie Plum.

**Standalones**
Individual books that are complete stories with no planned sequels or series connections. The premise should be fully resolved within one book with no loose threads requiring continuation. While the world could theoretically support more stories, each book is designed as a complete, independent experience. Think most literary fiction or one-off thrillers.

With this in mind, and including information from the [ANALYSIS], please generate 10 unique series premises for the genre(s) in the [SERIES METADATA] and type of series that can be used with the popular tropes as indicated in the [ANALYSIS].

Depending on the type of series, ensure the premises are able to support a series of {{ $('On form submission1').last().json['Number of Books in Series'] }} books.

No incest or incest-adjacent romantic relationships, please! This includes distant cousins!

Ensure each series premise includes the Essential Elements for Series Premises:

{{ $json.content }}

=[BEGIN SYSTEM]
{{ $('System Instructions').last().json.content }}
[END SYSTEM]

[BEGIN INSTRUCTIONS]
Evaluate each of the [PREMISES] based on the following criteria: 
Originality.
Emotional impact.
Feasibility to develop into a full series of {{ $('On form submission1').last().json['Number of Books in Series'] }} books.
Reader appeal (Meets reader expectations).

No incest or incest-adjacent romantic relationships, please! This includes distant cousins!

Assign a score from 1 to 10 for each criterion and include a short explanation for each score.

Identify the highest-scoring remixed and refined premise with a brief explanation of the market potential.

Based on the scores and evaluations, which premise stands out the most?

Explain why this premise is the best choice and suggest any final improvements.

[END INSTRUCTIONS]
[BEGIN BOOK DATA]
{{ $('Get Series Data').last().json.content }}
[END BOOK DATA]

[BEGIN PREMISES]
{{ $json.text }}
[END PREMISES]

=[BEGIN SYSTEM]
{{ $('System Instructions').last().json.content }}
[END SYSTEM]
{{ $('Get Series Data1').last().json.content }}
[BEGIN SERIES PREMISES]
{{ $('Get Series Premises1').last().json.content }}
[END SERIES PREMISES]
[BEGIN CHARACTER ROSTER]
{{ $('Get Roster').last().json.content }}
[END CHARACTER ROSTER]

[BEGIN SERIES DOSSIER TEMPLATE]
{{ $json.content }}
[END SERIES DOSSIER TEMPLATE]

[BEGIN INSTRUCTIONS]
Using the [SERIES METADATA]and the chosen [SERIES PREMISES], please provide a detailed Series Dossier using the provided template. This Dossier should contain all the relevant series information and can be referenced by the author when they are drafting each book in the series. Make sure to provide information to support all {{ $('Store Form Data').last().json['Number of Books in Series'] }} books in the series. NOTE: Pay close attention to the genre expectations.

DO NOT ABBREVIATE THE RESPONSE; INCLUDE DETAILS FOR ALL {{ $('Store Form Data').last().json['Number of Books in Series'] }} BOOKS IN THE SERIES.
[END INSTRUCTIONS]

[END SYSTEM]

{{ $('Get Series Premises').last().json.content }}

[BEGIN INSTRUCTIONS]

This series is a {{ $('On form submission1').last().json['Type of Project'] }}.

**Dynamic/Sequential Series (same characters)**
A multi-book series following the same core characters through an overarching plot that develops across all books. Each book advances the main story arc while containing its own complete plot. The premise must have sufficient complexity, escalating stakes, and character growth potential to sustain the planned number of books. Think Harry Potter or The Hunger Games.

**Anthology Series (interconnected different characters)**
A series set in the same world/universe where each book features different main characters, but all stories are interconnected through setting, themes, or background events. The premise must establish a rich world with multiple story possibilities and natural connection points between books. Each book stands alone but enriches the overall series experience. Think Black Dagger Brotherhood or Discworld.

**Static Series (character driven)**
A series featuring the same main character(s) in episodic adventures where each book is self-contained with minimal overarching plot development. The premise should focus on a compelling protagonist with a repeatable story framework (detective solving cases, spy on missions). Character relationships may evolve, but the core premise remains stable. Think Jack Reacher or Stephanie Plum.

**Standalones**
Individual books that are complete stories with no planned sequels or series connections. The premise should be fully resolved within one book with no loose threads requiring continuation. While the world could theoretically support more stories, each book is designed as a complete, independent experience. Think most literary fiction or one-off thrillers.

With this in mind, and including information from the [ANALYSIS] and [SERIES PREMISE], please generate 20 unique book premises for the series. Each premise should reflect the type of project. For example, Anthology Series should have the same series setting, but different main characters for each book, while Dynamic/Sequential Series have persistent main characters from book to book and the overarching plot elements from the [SERIES PREMISE] can be incorporated to reach a satisfying series plot conclusion.

We will select the {{ $('On form submission1').last().json['Number of Books in Series'] }} best of these premises for our final book premises, so they should all be related and feasibly exist within the same series.

No incest or incest-adjacent romantic relationships, please! This includes distant cousins!

Ensure each premise includes: 
A central conflict and high emotional stakes.
Trending tropes for guaranteed appeal.
Fresh twists to stand out in a saturated market.
Emotional beats and stakes that resonate with readers.
Market alignment with the identified trends.

Output: A list of compelling premises that include: 
The central conflict that sets up the [genre] dynamic.
A hook that makes it irresistible.
Characters readers can root for.
List of Major Tropes
Universal Fantasy Appeal

With this in mind, please evaluate each of the [BOOK PREMISES] based on the following criteria:

Each premise should reflect the type of project. [END INSTRUCTIONS]
